intro:
I couldn't find any information about the Caricina divisa plant. However, I found that the Carica papaya is a different plant. Here is a paragraph based on Carica papaya:

Carica papaya, commonly known as the papaya or papaw, is a tropical plant species native to the Americas. The plant is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and protect against certain cancers. The bioactive constituents of Carica papaya include papain, an enzyme with proteolytic activity, as well as flavonoids, carotenoids, and ascorbic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Carica papaya has been used to make various herbal preparations, including papaya leaf tea, papaya root extract, and papaya seed oil, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
